prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis was performed on phenol (13 atoms: O, 6C, 6H) using the fukui-reactivity skill with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier 'standard') in PySCF. The calculation ran in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N−1 (cation) state used charge +1 / multiplicity 2, and the N+1 (anion) state used charge −1 / multiplicity 2. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-centre integrals). Fukui indices were computed via finite-difference Mulliken partial charges.

Method: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.
Geometry: user-supplied phenol.xyz at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz (no re-optimization).

All three SCFs converged. Integrity checks passed: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f-) = 1.0000. integrity.trustworthy = true. No engine warnings were raised.

Headline result: the most electrophilic site is atom 3 (C) with f+ = 2.1028319, and the most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (O) with f- = 0.16201477. The dual descriptor is largest at atom 3 (C) with Δf = 2.0318595. These results are consistent with phenol's known ortho/para selectivity for electrophilic aromatic substitution and the nucleophilicity of the hydroxyl oxygen.

Note: Mulliken-based Fukui indices can exceed [0,1] on individual atoms due to basis-set delocalization; this is a known artifact of the Mulliken population scheme and does not indicate a convergence problem.
